anti_claims: This skill JSON seed does not flip source authority away from the current skill markdown projection.
Resolved operated-standard and acted-on-kind edges are structural route mappings, not proof the skill is complete or used correctly by agents.
Absent mechanism or concept neighbours are residual pressure, not evidence that no such neighbours exist.
Generated graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: mechanism or concept neighbours not named by typed source mapping
omission_receipt.reason: Skill instance preserves public-safe markdown source identity, digest, and typed route mapping only. JSON parity does not prove full skill-body completeness or runtime correctness.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Bind skill.uses mechanism and skill.applies concept edges only when source evidence names ids.

justification.summary: These three accepted standards all declare the same microcosm_runtime_organ author skill id. The target-shape tactic routing gate is the representative runtime-route anchor because it names a pre-execution organ, fixture manifest, paper module, validator contract, receipt contract, and public/private anti-claims; shared_standard_refs preserve the full accepted source set so resolving the skill id is route coverage, not per-organ runtime proof.

title: Microcosm Runtime Organ Author
triggers: An accepted runtime organ standard names the shared microcosm_runtime_organ author skill and needs source-backed organ admission rather than projection-only route pressure.

The organ can be authored from public fixture evidence, copied non-secret source modules, source digests, validator contracts, receipt expectations, paper-module capsules, mechanism refs, and explicit public/private boundaries.
workflow: Read the target standard JSON, organ atlas row, mechanism source, fixture manifest, source-module manifest, paper-module capsule, validator receipts, generated health, and relation registry before authoring.

Admit only public-safe runtime-organ fields: source refs, fixture refs, mechanism refs, paper-module refs, validator refs, receipt expectations, authority boundary, public/private boundary, and anti-claims.

Regenerate the skill corpus and doctrine lattice; keep theorem correctness, provider authority, private equivalence, release authority, and whole-organ completion as explicit non-claims unless source evidence proves them.
